mnemonics: C.P.U. Ticks Per Instruction: Cycles Per Unit (of) Time, Instructions Counted
Hertz High, Performance Fly: Higher clock speed (Hz) means faster processing.
keyPoints: The clock cycle is the fundamental unit of time for a processor; one oscillation of the clock signal.
Clock frequency, measured in Hertz (Hz), is the inverse of the clock period (time for one cycle).
CPU performance (execution time) is calculated as: (CPU Clock Cycles) × (Clock Cycle Time).
commonMistakes: Assuming a higher clock frequency always means better overall performance, ignoring other factors like instructions per cycle (IPC).
Confusing clock frequency (e.g., 3.5 GHz) with processor speed or throughput in operations per second.
